title: Strategy Synthesis Schema
description: Schema for the strategy_synthesis artifact produced by build-strategy-synthesis. Synthesizes store_profile, market_intelligence, brand_positioning, and competitor_clusters into an actionable strategic artifact for downstream planning phases.

strategic_summary.description: 2-4 sentence synthesis of the store's strategic situation: where it stands, what the market looks like, and what the core strategic challenge is.

growth_thesis.description: Core hypothesis about how this store will grow. LLM-synthesized from all upstream artifacts.

rationale.description: Why this growth thesis applies to this store given the market, brand, and competitive context.

depends_on.description: Key assumptions or conditions that must hold for this thesis to be valid.

positioning_focus.description: The primary positioning angle to pursue — synthesized from brand_positioning + competitive landscape.

primary_angle.description: The single strongest positioning angle (e.g., 'specialist authority in B2B workplace supplies').

differentiating_from.description: What clusters or competitor archetypes this positioning explicitly differentiates from.

supported_by.description: Upstream signal sources that support this focus (e.g., 'brand_positioning.brand_role=specialist', 'competitor_clusters: no specialist cluster present').

hypothesis.description: What could constitute a durable competitive advantage.

evidence_basis.description: Which upstream signals support this hypothesis.

risk.description: What could undermine this moat hypothesis.

moat_hypotheses.description: Hypotheses about durable competitive advantage. All speculative — require human validation.

opportunity.description: Specific opportunity identified from upstream artifacts.

source.description: Which upstream artifact this opportunity was derived from.

phase_implications.description: Which downstream planning phases are most affected by this opportunity.

opportunity_priorities.description: Top prioritized opportunities synthesized from all upstream artifacts.

risk.description: Specific risk identified from upstream artifacts.

mitigation_hint.description: Possible mitigation direction. Not a prescription — planning hypothesis only.

risk_priorities.description: Prioritized risks synthesized from all upstream artifacts.

message.description: Core message that should be communicated.

audience.description: Primary audience segment this message is for.

messaging_priorities.description: Priority messages derived from brand positioning and competitive context.

implication.description: What the synthesis means for product or offer strategy.

action_hint.description: A directional suggestion. Planning hypothesis — requires human review.

offer_implications.description: Implications for product, offer, and assortment strategy.

implication.description: Go-to-market implication derived from the strategic synthesis.

gtm_implications.description: Go-to-market implications by channel. Inform downstream gtm-plan phase.

validation_questions.description: Questions that require human validation before acting on this synthesis. Surfaces assumptions and unknown states.

primary_gaps.description: Key data gaps that limit confidence.

review_required.description: Whether human review is required before using this artifact downstream.

basis_artifacts.description: Which upstream artifacts contributed to this synthesis.

domain.description: Which domain this unknown belongs to (e.g., 'catalog', 'audience', 'competition').

unknown.description: What is not known or assumed — carried forward from upstream phases.

impact.description: How much this unknown could affect the synthesis quality.

source_artifact.description: Which upstream artifact flagged this unknown.

unknown_states.description: Unknown states carried forward from upstream phases. Explicitly surfaced rather than silently assumed.

notes.description: Free-form notes about this synthesis. Includes evidence boundary disclaimer.

_meta.description: Optional artifact metadata envelope (populated by runtime).
